title: Pad Thai / Noodle Sauce on Carpet
timeEstimate: 30–60 minutes; 4-component combo + carpet pad-saturation risk; wool = pro from minute one
stainChemistry: Pad Thai on carpet stacks the four-component combo (tamarind tannin + fish-sauce protein + caramelizing sugar + peanut/chili oil) with carpet-specific challenges: pad saturation (drives sticky sauce into the foam below where it wicks back as a permanent ring), wool carpet's banned-tools list (no broad-spectrum enzymes, no peroxide, no alkaline), and the impossibility of fully drying the carpet pile. Recurring traps: hot water (caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin all at once), alkaline cleaners as first step (darken tannin permanently), pour-on saturation (drives sauce into the pad). Wool carpet = pro from minute one. Synthetic carpet = home protocol with strict damp-cloth-only application.
whyThisWorks: Spoon-and-blot lifts solid bulk before chemistry. Cold damp-cloth blot dissolves sugar without saturating the pad. Cool enzyme pretreat handles fish-sauce protein. Dawn damp-cloth strips peanut and chili oil. Vinegar disrupts tamarind tannin. 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet handles residual color. Weighted paper towels overnight prevent wick-back from any moisture that reached the pad. Damp-cloth-only application throughout — never pour.
scienceNote: Mechanism (pro-facing): pad-thai is tamarind tannin polyphenols + fish-sauce proteins + sucrose/palm sugar + peanut + chili oil with capsanthin pigment. Pro uses cold damp-cloth flush + protein spotter + NSD oil-strip + acid-side tannin spotter + 3% H₂O₂. Wool carpet requires urease-only enzyme + extreme dilution.
defaultAssumption: Thai food / noodle takeout spill on synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) home carpet. Wool carpet = pro from minute one. Pad bleed-through = restoration territory.

spottingProtocol.instruction: Wool carpet = pro from minute one. Synthetic carpet: scoop solid noodles, sauce, peanuts into a garbage bag with a spoon — never rub.

Damp white cloth with COLD water. Press the spot, lift, rotate to clean section, press again. NEVER pour water — pad saturation drives sauce into foam where it wicks back. 2–3 cycles to dissolve sugar.

spottingProtocol.equipment: OxiClean Versatile / Biz / pet enzyme in COOL water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat in COOL water per label. Apply with damp cloth — NEVER pour. Cover with damp towel to prevent drying. 15–20 min. Cool damp-cloth rinse.
Dawn worked into a damp cloth — never pour. Press the spot, soft toothbrush. 5 min. Damp-cloth rinse.

spottingProtocol.equipment: 1 tbsp white vinegar + 1 cup cool water on damp cloth
spottingProtocol.instruction: Vinegar + cool water on damp cloth, 5 min. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before peroxide.
Hidden carpet area test before peroxide on dyed carpet. Color transfers = skip peroxide.

spottingProtocol.technique: Bounded oxidation on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet
spottingProtocol.instruction: Hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet only. 3% peroxide damp cloth 5–10 min for residual color. NEVER mix with chlorine, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners.
Stack paper towels on the spot, weight overnight. Fan in the morning until fully dry. Prevents brown shadow from wicking back from any moisture that reached the pad.

steps: 1. Wool carpet = pro from minute one.
3. Cold damp-cloth blot 2–3 passes — never pour.
4. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water on damp cloth, 15–20 min.
6. Vinegar damp-cloth 5 min — rinse before peroxide.
7. Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed carpet.
8. 3% peroxide damp cloth on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet.
products: Enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile / Biz / pet enzyme)
professionalProtocol.temperature: cool throughout — heat sets every component
warnings: Wool carpet = pro from minute one
ORDER MATTERS — sugar flush, then protein, then oil, then tannin acid, then peroxide
NEVER hot water — caramelizes sugar + cooks protein + sets tannin
NEVER alkaline cleaners as first step — DARKEN tannin permanently
NEVER pour water or chemistry on carpet — damp-cloth only
NEVER hot extractor / steam — locks residue
Hidden peroxide test on dyed carpet

homeSolutions.agent: Wool Carpet Goes to Pro — Synthetic Carpet Has the 5-Step Sequence
homeSolutions.instruction: Pad Thai on carpet is a four-component combo (sugar + protein + oil + tannin) with carpet-specific challenges. Wool carpet = pro from minute one (broad-spectrum enzymes damage wool, peroxide yellows wool). Synthetic carpet (nylon, polyester, olefin) follows: solid bulk → cold blot (sugar) → cool enzyme (protein) → Dawn (oil) → vinegar (tannin) → peroxide on hidden-test-passed → weighted towels. Three hard rules: NO hot water (caramelizes sugar, cooks protein, sets tannin), NO alkaline cleaners as first step (darken tannin permanently), NO pour-on water (saturates the pad).
Spoon or plastic card to scoop noodles, peanuts, sauce into a garbage bag. Don't wipe or rub — that drives the sticky sauce into the carpet pile.

Damp a white cloth with COLD water. Press the spot, lift, rotate to a clean section, press again. NEVER pour water on the carpet — pour-on saturation drives sauce into the pad below where you can't reach it. 2 to 3 cycles to dissolve sugar.

homeSolutions.agent: Enzyme Pretreat — Cool Water, Damp-Cloth Only
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve enzyme pretreat (OxiClean Versatile, Biz, pet enzyme) in COOL water per label. Apply with a damp cloth — NEVER pour. Cover with a damp towel to prevent drying. 15 to 20 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse.

homeSolutions.agent: Dawn Damp Cloth — Strip Peanut + Chili Oil
homeSolutions.instruction: Dawn worked into a damp cloth, soft toothbrush, 5 minutes. Strips peanut and chili oil. Damp-cloth rinse — never pour.

homeSolutions.agent: White Vinegar Damp Cloth — Tannin Acid
homeSolutions.instruction: Mix 1 tablespoon white vinegar with 1 cup cool water. Apply with a damp cloth, 5 minutes. Damp-cloth rinse THOROUGHLY before peroxide.
In a hidden carpet area, dab 3% peroxide on a white cloth, press 2 minutes. Color transfers = skip peroxide.
Hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet only: 3% drugstore peroxide on a damp cloth, press 5 to 10 minutes, blot.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix peroxide with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or alkaline cleaners — toxic fumes.

homeSolutions.agent: Weighted Paper Towels Overnight Plus Fan
homeSolutions.instruction: Stack paper towels on the spot, weight with a heavy book, leave overnight. Fan-dry in the morning. Pulls residual moisture and prevents brown shadow from wicking back up from the pad.

steps: 1. Wool carpet = pro from minute one. NO alkaline cleaners. NO hot water. NO pouring water.
3. Cold damp-cloth blot 2–3 passes for sugar.
4. Enzyme pretreat in COOL water on damp cloth, 15–20 min — protein FIRST.
6. Diluted white vinegar damp-cloth 5 min — rinse before peroxide.
7. Hidden-area peroxide test on dyed carpet.
8. 3% peroxide damp cloth on hidden-test-passed synthetic carpet.

diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop on wool carpet (pro). Stop on pad bleed-through (restoration). Stop if hot water / steam / hot vacuum was used (often locks every component). Stop if alkaline cleaner was used as first step. Stop if hidden peroxide test fails on dyed carpet.

NEVER hot water — locks every component
NEVER alkaline cleaners — DARKEN tannin permanently
NEVER hot extractor / steam — locks residue

neverDo: Never wool carpet at home — pro from minute one
Never hot water on pad-thai + carpet
Never alkaline cleaners as first step on tannin
Never pour water on carpet — pad saturation
Never hot extractor / steam — locks residue
Never mix peroxide + chlorine + ammonia + OxiClean
fiberSensitivities: Synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) carpet: tolerates damp-cloth 5-step sequence
Wool carpet: pro from minute one — broad-spectrum enzymes damage wool, peroxide yellows wool
Dyed carpet: hidden peroxide test mandatory
Carpet pad: cannot be cleaned in place — saturation = pad replacement

No pour-on water — pad saturation drives sauce into foam

escalation.when: Wool carpet from minute one. Hot water / steam / hot vacuum already used. Alkaline cleaner used as first step. Pour-on water saturation visible. Pad bleed-through (restoration territory). 3 home cycles plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Pad Thai / noodle sauce on [synthetic / wool] carpet, age [fresh / hours / days], home-treated with [solid lift + cold damp-cloth blot + cool enzyme + Dawn + vinegar + 3% peroxide on hidden-test-passed + weighted towels overnight]. No hot, no alkaline, no pouring. [Residual sticky shadow / pad bleed-through suspected]."
escalation.specialistType: professional carpet cleaner with combination-stain (sugar + protein + oil + tannin) chemistry; specialty wool carpet cleaner if wool

contextQuestions: Synthetic (nylon / polyester / olefin) carpet or wool?
Pad Thai, lo mein, drunken noodles, or other Asian sauce?
Has hot water / steam / hot vacuum been used?
Has alkaline cleaner been used as first step?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Wool carpet protocol is pro because peroxide yellows wool and broad-spectrum enzymes damage the keratin. Sugar caramelization plus pour-on saturation are the two silent failure modes — both lock the stain in ways pro chemistry can't easily reverse. Damp-cloth-only application is non-negotiable on carpet.
